_metadata.title: Non-structural component mitigation map (Philippine school portfolio)
_metadata.description: The thesis's SECOND mitigation layer (beyond the structural FRP retrofit of the non-ductile RCMRF): broadly-applied non-structural equipment upgrades whose purpose is to reduce INJURIES from falling hazards (Jeswani 2021 Ch 6.2.4 Table 6-3; Ch 8.3 'Short-Term Non-Structural Mitigations'). Each acceleration- or drift-sensitive non-structural component is replaced by its anchored/braced/seismic counterpart (higher fragility capacity AND, for fixtures/electronics, no casualty consequence). This is a COMPONENT-LEVEL fragility/consequence swap — NOT new EDPs and NOT a structural re-analysis (HARD SCOPE compliant). It changes the Pelicun damage sample (which drives the non-collapse component injury pathway in casualties.py) and the component repair loss; it does NOT change collapse probability (that is the structural FRP layer's job).
_metadata.source_thesis: Jeswani, K. K. (2021), MASc thesis, University of Toronto. Table 6-3 'Simulated Component Mitigation Strategies' (printed p.128; docs/thesis/06_vulnerability_loss_estimation.md:204-214); Table 6-4 archetype/mitigated-counterpart assignments (printed p.129-130); Ch 8.3 'Short-Term Non-Structural Mitigations' (08_conclusions.md:87): brace ceilings, anchor/safety-wire fixtures (fans/lights), secure desktop electronics, anchor wall-mounted AC units. Thesis result: 'Non-structural mitigation measures neutralized non-collapse injuries' (06:448, 460); injury rate 14.5%->1.1% (Makati), 9.1%->2.3% (QC) (08:37).
_metadata.mechanism: At the Pelicun asset-marginals build (building._build_cmp_marginals), every base component id that appears as a key in 'substitutions' is replaced by its mapped 'to' component id (a stronger/anchored variant that already exists in fragility.csv + consequence_repair.csv), and every component id in 'removed' is dropped from the marginals entirely (thesis: electronics are 'removed item from mitigated models (simulate no damage/casualty)'). The mitigated counterparts (PH.NS.CLG.BR, PH.NS.FIX.SE, PH.NS.CHB.SR, PH.NS.CHB.PR) carry NO non-collapse injury consequence in casualty_consequences.json (FIX.SE, CHB.*R) or a much higher acceleration capacity (CLG.BR DS3 theta 2.07 g vs CLG.NS 1.31 g), so the non-collapse injury pathway is suppressed for the upgraded components. Quantities, locations, directions and all other (structural) components are unchanged.
_metadata.scope: Applied PORTFOLIO-WIDE for the upgraded component classes (every building that carries one of these acceleration/drift-sensitive non-structural components gets the upgrade), per the thesis's broad non-structural programme. This is NOT limited to the FRP-retrofitted archetypes — the thesis applied the non-structural upgrades across the portfolio (Table 6-4 marks the mitigated counterpart '✓' for the C1-M / PTC1-M / C1-L primary archetypes that carry these components). The structural FRP layer (C1-M (Pre/Lo) -> C1-M (Pre/Lo) FRP) is a SEPARATE map (portfolio.MITIGATION_RETROFIT_MAP) and is applied simultaneously in the mitigated run.
_metadata.provenance_overall: high for the substitution targets (Table 6-3 is an explicit existing->upgraded component table; all target ids already exist in fragility.csv / consequence_repair.csv with thesis provenance). The braced-ceiling, seismic-fixture and reinforced-CHB fragilities are the thesis's own Table D-4 values; the electronics 'removed' treatment is verbatim from Table 6-3.
_metadata.generated_by: OPUS mitigation-correction agent 2026-06-27 (extracted from thesis Table 6-3 + Table 6-4; corrects the prior agent's structural-only framing). Data-driven: code reads this file, never hardcodes the map.

PH.NS.CLG.NS.label: Suspended Ceiling; Non-Seismic -> Braced
PH.NS.CLG.NS.upgrade_activities: Partial removal of existing ceiling; install compression struts and diagonal splay wires; install wall bracket (one end fixed, other free) if none present.
PH.NS.CLG.NS.effect: Acceleration capacity raised (DS3 PFA theta 1.31 g -> 2.07 g); fewer ceiling sections reach the casualty DS3 (injury theta 0.10 over 250 SF). Loss impact marginal (same repair consequence).

PH.NS.FIX.NS.label: Ceiling Fixtures; Non-Seismic -> Braced/Seismic (safety-wired)
PH.NS.FIX.NS.upgrade_activities: Check existing anchorage, move away from MRF beams if necessary; install additional safety cables (~25% of new lighting fixture cost). Safety-wire fans/lights, phase out pendant fixtures.
PH.NS.FIX.NS.effect: Seismic/safety-wired fixtures carry NO non-collapse casualty (PH.NS.FIX.SE is absent from casualty_consequences.json noncollapse table), vs PH.NS.FIX.NS injury theta 0.10 over 50 SF per damaged unit. This is the DOMINANT non-collapse injury driver in the school archetypes, so its neutralisation is the headline injury benefit (thesis: 'non-structural mitigation neutralized non-collapse injuries').
PH.NS.FIX.NS.source: Table 6-3 (p.128); Table 6-2 (p.121) 'Ceiling Fixtures; Seismic ... no casualty'; fragility PH.NS.FIX.SE Table D-4 (p.298).

PH.NS.CHB.SU.label: CHB Wall; Solid; Unreinforced -> Reinforced
PH.NS.CHB.SU.upgrade_activities: Saw-cut isolation at wall-ends, re-tooth, polypropylene expansion joint, post-installed anchors into frame, welded-wire-mesh both sides, new mortar, paint.
PH.NS.CHB.SU.effect: Reinforced CHB has higher drift capacity (DS1 PIDR 0.13% -> 0.23%, DS2 0.31% -> 0.67%) and NO heavy-damage DS3 -> NO non-collapse injury consequence (PH.NS.CHB.SR carries no casualty DS in casualty_consequences.json). Reduces the secondary CHB injury contribution and 'no global collapse' of infill.
PH.NS.CHB.SU.note: CHB casualty consequences are approximated in the thesis (Table D-4 footnote).

PH.NS.CHB.PU.label: CHB with Doors/Windows; Unreinforced -> Reinforced
PH.NS.CHB.PU.upgrade_activities: As for solid CHB (welded-wire-mesh, anchors, expansion joint), preserving openings.
PH.NS.CHB.PU.effect: Reinforced perforated CHB: higher drift capacity, casualty DS3/DS4 removed -> no non-collapse injury consequence.
PH.NS.CHB.PU.note: CHB casualty consequences are approximated in the thesis (Table D-4 footnote).

PH.NS.ELEC.DT.label: Desktop Electronics
PH.NS.ELEC.DT.upgrade_activities: Attach safety fasteners / heavy-duty straps so the unit does not fall.
PH.NS.ELEC.DT.effect: Thesis: 'Removed item from mitigated models (simulate no damage/casualty)'. Dropped from the mitigated marginals -> no damage, no non-collapse injury (was injury theta 0.10 over 16 SF).

PH.NS.ELEC.WM.label: Wall-Mounted Electronics (AC units / TVs)
PH.NS.ELEC.WM.upgrade_activities: Attach additional angled brackets to anchor wall-mounted AC units.
PH.NS.ELEC.WM.effect: Thesis: 'Removed item from mitigated models (simulate no damage/casualty)'. Dropped from the mitigated marginals -> no damage, no non-collapse injury/fatality (was the only non-collapse FATALITY-bearing NSC: injury 0.20 / fatality 0.10 over 16 SF, high PFA capacity 2.5 g so rarely fired).
